content.full_text:
Supreme Court of California
303 SECOND STREET, SOUTH TOWER
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94107


MALCOLM M LUCAS
CHIEF JUSTICE

April 3, 1991


Mr. Mark Morodomi
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

Dear Mark:

I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation
today regarding the ability of Chief Justice Lucas to
receive honoraria pursuant to current FPPC guidelines.
You have informed us that although the rules governing
honoraria and gifts were changed in 1989 to prohibit
"elected officials" from receiving any gift totaling more
than $250, or any honoraria totaling more than $1,000,
these rules do not apply to appointed judges or justices.
Hence, it is our understanding that Chief Justice Lucas
may receive honoraria (in any amount), as long as he
discloses the total amount received for the year on his
annual FPPC form 721. You agreed today to confirm the
above information by letter.

I appreciate your assistance in this matter and
look forward to hearing from you soon. Should you have
any questions, please do not hesitate to call me at
415/396-9415.

Cordially,

Abbe W. McCall
Abbe W. McCall
Staff Attorney for
Chief Justice Malcolm M. Lucas

AWM:mja
